name: python-pptx (PowerPoint File Generation)

performance.notes: Pure Python; performance acceptable for typical decks

agent_readiness.notes: Read and write .pptx PowerPoint files. Presentation, Slide, Shape, TextFrame objects. Add slides from layouts. Text boxes with paragraphs and runs. Charts: bar, line, pie (via chart XML). Images. Tables. Slide masters and layouts. Fill, font, color properties. Shape positioning via Inches()/Pt() units. Template-based workflows (modify existing .pptx). AI-generated slide decks from data. Read existing presentations for data extraction. Widely used for automated reporting and AI slide generation.

gotchas: Unit system (EMU, Inches, Pt) is confusing at first
Limited chart types compared to native PowerPoint
Some PowerPoint features not accessible via API
best_when: Generating automated PowerPoint reports from data
AI-assisted slide deck creation from structured data
Extracting text/data from existing PowerPoint files
avoid_when: Non-PowerPoint output (use Mermaid or d2 for diagrams)
summary: python-pptx reads and writes PowerPoint .pptx files. Add slides, text boxes, images, charts, tables. Template-based workflow for modifying existing presentations. Used for AI-generated slide decks and automated reporting. Unit system (Inches/Pt) requires adjustment. Best for automated PowerPoint generation from data or template-based slide modification. Complex animations not supported.
